query: When does a change of degree programme actually become active?

queries: When does a change of degree programme actually become active?

retrieved.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
retrieved.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

retrieved.title: Career orientation and educational choice
retrieved.text: makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least three years. Bear in mind that you will deal with topics of this degree programme for the next 3 to 5 years . - Your curriculum (or study plan) is a helpful tool for choosing your degree programme. Each degree programme has its own curriculum which provides you with information on the programme structure, contents and the course of study. On the details page of each degree programme you can find a link to the curriculum. - Get information about possible deadlines, requirements or entrance exam procedures in time. For example, if you would like to study Psychology or Journalism and Communication Studies, you have to participate in an entrance exam procedure. Also, different admission deadlines apply. If you would like to study Sport Science, physical fitness is very important: There is a supplementary examination which you have to complete before you start with your degree programme. Some degree programmes require (previous) knowledge in Latin or Greek. © Universität Wien/Martin Sowa

retrieved.title: Change of degree programme
retrieved.text:
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

retrieved.text:
Information regarding the change from a non-degree to a degree programme after:

- [Completing the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)
- [Receiving the secondary-school leaving certificate "Nachmatura" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/)
- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
retrieved.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

retrieved.title: Change of master programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the curriculum of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

retrieved.title: Change of master programme
retrieved.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Dean/Dean's Office Together with the vice dean, the dean presides over a faculty or department. The dean’s office is the central contact point for all administrative and organisational issues of the respective faculty. Related entries: - [Faculty](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29992&cHash=9834ee1cb244b7812bac8dd0cb0201a5) - ["Promotion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31237&cHash=e793360b6437022540232d8d31aeb91e) - Degree programme If you have been admitted to a bachelor, diploma, master, Magister, doctoral or PhD degree programme, you are studying a so-called "degree programme". There is a curriculum for these programmes. After completing your degree programme, you will receive a title (= academic degree). If you do not (yet) fulfil the admission requirements for the degree programme or are interested in attending individual courses, you can be admitted to the non-degree programme. Related Links: - [Admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/#c510435) - [Curriculum (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/#c946981) Related entries: - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - Degree programme code Every degree programme in Austria has its own degree programme code, which makes it clearly identifiable. Your degree programme code is specified in u:space in the section "Studies" > "Study Overview", as well as on your student record sheet in the section "Personal Matters" > "My Documents". - Description of qualifications For some master programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your

retrieved.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

Get to know the main building, find out about studying abroad or discover the library’s services. Workshops, guided tours, and other information events starting in mid-September offer skills and tips for your studies.

New events are being added continuously.

Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

reranked.title: Career orientation and educational choice
reranked.text: makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least three years. Bear in mind that you will deal with topics of this degree programme for the next 3 to 5 years . - Your curriculum (or study plan) is a helpful tool for choosing your degree programme. Each degree programme has its own curriculum which provides you with information on the programme structure, contents and the course of study. On the details page of each degree programme you can find a link to the curriculum. - Get information about possible deadlines, requirements or entrance exam procedures in time. For example, if you would like to study Psychology or Journalism and Communication Studies, you have to participate in an entrance exam procedure. Also, different admission deadlines apply. If you would like to study Sport Science, physical fitness is very important: There is a supplementary examination which you have to complete before you start with your degree programme. Some degree programmes require (previous) knowledge in Latin or Greek. © Universität Wien/Martin Sowa

reranked.title: Change of degree programme
reranked.text:
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

reranked.title: Change of degree programme
reranked.text:
Information regarding the change from a non-degree to a degree programme after:

- [Completing the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)
- [Receiving the secondary-school leaving certificate "Nachmatura" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/)
- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). - [Information regarding working languages](/en/entrance-exam/transcultural-communication/working-languages/)

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
reranked.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

reranked.title: Change of master programme
reranked.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the curriculum of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

reranked.text: to the degree programme expires. A resumption of the same degree programme is only possible after two academic years (= four semesters). Students with psychological/physical impairments are exempt and don't have to obtain the minimum number of credits. Related Links: - [Minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) - [About the admission](/en/admission-procedure/#c510447) Related entries: - [ECTS credits](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30855&cHash=fbeb490cf5731d782a7600223421c9d7) - [Prüfungsaktiv - examination activity](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=40196&cHash=c8b5a0cf3da52a9cb8416c0fca47513a) - Mobility programmes The University of Vienna offers a range of mobility programmes. This means that students have the possibility to spend one or more semesters at a different university within Europe or beyond, gather experience living in another country and improve their foreign language skills. Students from abroad can also study at the University of Vienna by means of a mobility programme. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [CEEPUS](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29987&cHash=72c1e3c31b64c2842340511847949735) - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06) - [Exchange programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30818&cHash=44ce8bc355947818e4e6cf53f6d144f8) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - Module The curricula of bachelor’s and master’s programmes are divided into modules. Each module specifies the learning outcomes that students have to achieve. You can view the structure of your degree programme and the corresponding modules in your record of examinations in u:space. Related Links: - [Record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) Related entries: - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - [Module/subject exam](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31193&cHash=3e6c779e9e6978b2d98ec222925158b0) - [Record of examinations](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31245&cHash=84f002403c7d9577c0489d8f460cdcaa) - [Stage of the degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31266&cHash=a31be547fd42def4414091d3fe87e1d1) - [Curriculum item](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31282&cHash=0e25d76f4b25fac6b1f9a38f29f70bf0) - Module/subject exam A module exam (or subject exam) is an exam covering the contents of

reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: the academic degree. You also receive your diploma supplement. The aim of the bilingual diploma supplement (in German/English) is to enhance the transparency and recognition of higher education degrees. Your diploma supplement contains information about the type and level of your degree, the degree programme you completed (requirements, course of studies, grading scale) as well as as standardised information about the higher education system. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/home) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Diploma Supplement](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30852&cHash=e0f7f659728ec124c1ab41f985dc9792) - Change of degree programme Would you like to change your degree programme at the University of Vienna? Apply for admission to the new degree programme in u:space. Please note the application deadlines! Then write a message to the Admission Office via the Service Desk and have your previous degree programme closed. Related Links: - [Information on the change of degree programme](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/) - Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna The closure of a degree programme is not subject to any deadlines at the University of Vienna. Therefore, you can request the closure of a degree programme throughout the year. If you request to close all your degree programmes, this is referred to as “withdrawal from the university”. Related Links: - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawal from university](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) Related entries: - [Confirmation of withdrawal ("Abgangsbescheinigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30169&cHash=4d6b9c8665691a99bc99014b4a29d313) - [Termination of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30831&cHash=b781b61903ed2560155fd624dbf8e70b) - Colloquium A colloquium can

Related Links: - [Complementary degree programme in Teacher Education (only in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassungsverfahren/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium) - [Master programme in Teacher Education (only in German)](https://ssc-lehrerinnenbildung.univie.ac.at/studienangebot/masterstudium/) - Completion of a degree programme As soon as you have successfully passed all courses specified in your curriculum, you have completed your degree programme. reranked.title: Change of master programme
reranked.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Dean/Dean's Office Together with the vice dean, the dean presides over a faculty or department. The dean’s office is the central contact point for all administrative and organisational issues of the respective faculty. Related entries: - [Faculty](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29992&cHash=9834ee1cb244b7812bac8dd0cb0201a5) - ["Promotion"](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31237&cHash=e793360b6437022540232d8d31aeb91e) - Degree programme If you have been admitted to a bachelor, diploma, master, Magister, doctoral or PhD degree programme, you are studying a so-called "degree programme". There is a curriculum for these programmes. After completing your degree programme, you will receive a title (= academic degree). If you do not (yet) fulfil the admission requirements for the degree programme or are interested in attending individual courses, you can be admitted to the non-degree programme. Related Links: - [Admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/#c510435) - [Curriculum (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/#c946981) Related entries: - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - Degree programme code Every degree programme in Austria has its own degree programme code, which makes it clearly identifiable. Your degree programme code is specified in u:space in the section "Studies" > "Study Overview", as well as on your student record sheet in the section "Personal Matters" > "My Documents". - Description of qualifications For some master programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your

reranked.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

©

Get to know the main building, find out about studying abroad or discover the library’s services. Workshops, guided tours, and other information events starting in mid-September offer skills and tips for your studies.

selected.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
selected.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

selected.title: Career orientation and educational choice
selected.text: makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least three years. Bear in mind that you will deal with topics of this degree programme for the next 3 to 5 years . - Your curriculum (or study plan) is a helpful tool for choosing your degree programme. Each degree programme has its own curriculum which provides you with information on the programme structure, contents and the course of study. On the details page of each degree programme you can find a link to the curriculum. - Get information about possible deadlines, requirements or entrance exam procedures in time. For example, if you would like to study Psychology or Journalism and Communication Studies, you have to participate in an entrance exam procedure. Also, different admission deadlines apply. If you would like to study Sport Science, physical fitness is very important: There is a supplementary examination which you have to complete before you start with your degree programme. Some degree programmes require (previous) knowledge in Latin or Greek. © Universität Wien/Martin Sowa

selected.title: Change of degree programme
selected.text:
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

selected.title: Change of degree programme
selected.text:
Information regarding the change from a non-degree to a degree programme after:

- [Completing the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)
- [Receiving the secondary-school leaving certificate "Nachmatura" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/)
- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

selected.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
selected.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

selected.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
selected.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

assemble.context:
[1] Change of degree programme — General information regarding the change of a degree programme
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

[2] Career orientation and educational choice
makes you achieve your goal. Do not get yourself influenced too much by future job opportunities when making your decision - the labour market can change radically within the next 3 to 5 years. A degree programme takes at least three years. Bear in mind that you will deal with topics of this degree programme for the next 3 to 5 years . - Your curriculum (or study plan) is a helpful tool for choosing your degree programme. Each degree programme has its own curriculum which provides you with information on the programme structure, contents and the course of study. On the details page of each degree programme you can find a link to the curriculum. - Get information about possible deadlines, requirements or entrance exam procedures in time. For example, if you would like to study Psychology or Journalism and Communication Studies, you have to participate in an entrance exam procedure. Also, different admission deadlines apply. If you would like to study Sport Science, physical fitness is very important: There is a supplementary examination which you have to complete before you start with your degree programme. Some degree programmes require (previous) knowledge in Latin or Greek. © Universität Wien/Martin Sowa

[3] Change of degree programme
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

[5] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

[6] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — FAQ
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).
